title: valibot Is Trending: Here's What Programming Languages Developers Need to Know
description: valibot is trending on GitHub with 12.8M new stars this week. The modular and type safe schema library for validating structural data.

sourceData.name: valibot
sourceData.description: The modular and type safe schema library for validating structural data

bodyHtml:
valibot is making waves in the Programming Languages ecosystem this week, gaining 12.8MM weekly downloads. accruing over 8.8k stars on GitHub, this open-source project is rapidly becoming a go-to tool for developers worldwide.

⭐ 8.8kStars
📈 +12.8M/wkGrowth
🔀 352Forks
⚠️ 147Open Issues

Key Features

  • Written in JavaScript/TypeScript for maximum performance
  • Licensed under MIT — free to use and modify
  • Official website: https://valibot.dev
  • 🏷️ modular
  • 🏷️ typescript
  • 🏷️ schema
  • 🏷️ validation
  • 🏷️ parsing

Why valibot Is Trending

The Programming Languages landscape is constantly evolving, and valibot has emerged as one of the fastest-growing projects this week. Several factors contribute to its momentum:

  1. Growing community: With 12.8M new stars this week alone, developer adoption is accelerating rapidly.
  2. Active maintenance: The project sees regular updates, ensuring compatibility with the latest ecosystem changes.
  3. Practical utility: The modular and type safe schema library for validating structural data

If you're working in Programming Languages, this is definitely a project worth watching — and adopting.

Quick Start

Get started with valibot in minutes:

# Clone the repository git clone https://www.npmjs.com/package/valibot # Navigate to project cd valibot # Check the README for detailed setup instructions

For full documentation and advanced usage, visit the official GitHub repository.

Final Thoughts

valibot is a solid addition to any developer's toolkit. With its growing community, active development, and practical design, it's well worth your time to evaluate. Star it on GitHub, try it in a side project, and see if it fits your workflow.
